title: International Sports - The Kathmandu Post
summary: International Sports Bangladesh stand firm on India decision despite World Cup ultimatum By Reuters The ICC board has dismissed Bangladesh’s concerns regarding the safety of its players and fans in India, despite persistent political tensions between the two South Asian neighbours. Pakistan seek revenge, India plot a hat-trick of humiliation By Reuters The Indian juggernaut has crushed every oppos
content:
International Sports Bangladesh stand firm on India decision despite World Cup ultimatum By Reuters The ICC board has dismissed Bangladesh’s concerns regarding the safety of its players and fans in India, despite persistent political tensions between the two South Asian neighbours. Pakistan seek revenge, India plot a hat-trick of humiliation By Reuters The Indian juggernaut has crushed every opposition on their path before swaggering into the final of the eight-team tournament looking every inch the reigning 20-over world champions. Ngumoha in dream Premier League debut with late winner for Liverpool at Newcastle By Reuters Teenager Ngumoha snatches stoppage-time winner as Newcastle crumble despite valiant fight. Chinese soccer team train for inaugural World Humanoid Robot Games By Reuters The Games will bring together teams from more than 20 countries for events ranging from track and field to dance and martial arts, as well as practical applications such as industrial handling and medical services. German biathlete Dahlmeier dead after mountaineering accident, says Bild newspaper By Reuters The double Olympic champion started in her first world cup races in 2012-13 season and took the retirement from the competition in May 2019. England women to return home to heroes’ welcome after Euro 2025 win By Reuters The FA has urged fans to avoid going to the airport due to limited space and instead join Tuesday’s homecoming event in central London, which will feature live music and big screens showing tournament highlights Inter Miami acquire M Rodrigo De Paul from Atletico By Reuters In addition to the World Cup, De Paul helped the team win Copa America championships in 2021 and 2024. Australia beat West Indies by three wickets in fourth T20 By Reuters The visitors went on to clinch the win with four balls to spare and took a 4-0 lead in the series courtesy of Cameron Green's knock of 55 off 35 balls - his third half-century in the four matches. Women’s Euro exit shows once-mighty Germany battling to keep pace By Reuters Eight-time champions Germany have not won the tournament since 2013 in Stockholm, and in the meantime they have been reined in and passed by as other countries pour money into the development of the women’s game. England teammates sing praises of young saviour Agyemang By Reuters Agyemang is a skilled bulldozer of a striker with a nose for the net and no fear of battling for possession. England, Sweden are different teams than in Euro 2022 semi-final, says Williamson By Reuters Sweden dismantled plenty of people’s favourites Germany 4-1 to top Group C and Williamson said they had not been getting the respect they deserved in Switzerland. Switzerland cancel training session ahead of Spain clash due to illness By Reuters Less than half an hour before the session was due to begin, the Swiss football association messaged media covering the team on WhatsApp to say that it had been cancelled. Club World Cup serves as 2026 dry run amid heat and pitch concerns By Reuters A record 48 teams will descend on co-hosts United States, Canada and Mexico next year for the quadrennial spectacle, with 104 matches on the schedule, up from just 64 the last time the tournament was held in 2022 in Qatar. Red card decisive in 4-1 loss to Sweden, Germany coach says By Reuters Following a bright start, capped with a goal from Jule Brand, Germany were second best for most of the match at Zurich’s Letzigrund stadium. Towel thief Swiatek gets personalised Wimbledon souvenir By Reuters On Saturday, Wimbledon marked her love of the towels by presenting the new champion with a personalised purple and green version India’s top league ISL on hold, federation says By Reuters Talks to renew the 2010 agreement stalled after India’s Supreme Court asked the AIFF not to renew their deal with FSDL until its order, the federation said. Sinner seeks redemption, Alcaraz a three-peat in Wimbledon final showdown By Reuters There are many other plot lines but above all the showdown will help to cement a rivalry that could dominate tennis for a decade. Liverpool’s Diogo Jota mourned by family, Portugal’s PM in hometown wake By Reuters The death of forward Jota at the age of 28 has jolted the world of football, with messages of homage pouring in from former teammates, clubs, national leaders and fans. Italy’s Stano breaks 35-km race walk world record By Reuters The 33-year-old also won gold in 35-km race walk at the 2022 World Athletics Championships Barcelona claim 28th LaLiga title after 2-0 win at Espanyol By Reuters It caps a remarkable first season at Barca for coach Hansi Flick as his side have won the LaLiga and Copa del Rey double.

England women to return home to heroes’ welcome after Euro 2025 win By Reuters The FA has urged fans to avoid going to the airport due to limited space and instead join Tuesday’s homecoming event in central London, which will feature live music and big screens showing tournament highlights Inter Miami acquire M Rodrigo De Paul from Atletico By Reuters In addition to the World Cup, De Paul helped the team win Copa America championships in 2021 and 2024. Australia beat West Indies by three wickets in fourth T20 By Reuters The visitors went on to clinch the win with four balls to spare and took a 4-0 lead in the series courtesy of Cameron Green's knock of 55 off 35 balls - his third half-century in the four matches. England teammates sing praises of young saviour Agyemang By Reuters Agyemang is a skilled bulldozer of a striker with a nose for the net and no fear of battling for possession. England, Sweden are different teams than in Euro 2022 semi-final, says Williamson By Reuters Sweden dismantled plenty of people’s favourites Germany 4-1 to top Group C and Williamson said they had not been getting the respect they deserved in Switzerland. Switzerland cancel training session ahead of Spain clash due to illness By Reuters Less than half an hour before the session was due to begin, the Swiss football association messaged media covering the team on WhatsApp to say that it had been cancelled. Club World Cup serves as 2026 dry run amid heat and pitch concerns By Reuters A record 48 teams will descend on co-hosts United States, Canada and Mexico next year for the quadrennial spectacle, with 104 matches on the schedule, up from just 64 the last time the tournament was held in 2022 in Qatar. Sinner seeks redemption, Alcaraz a three-peat in Wimbledon final showdown By Reuters There are many other plot lines but above all the showdown will help to cement a rivalry that could dominate tennis for a decade. Liverpool’s Diogo Jota mourned by family, Portugal’s PM in hometown wake By Reuters The death of forward Jota at the age of 28 has jolted the world of football, with messages of homage pouring in from former teammates, clubs, national leaders and fans. Italy’s Stano breaks 35-km race walk world record By Reuters The 33-year-old also won gold in 35-km race walk at the 2022 World Athletics Championships Barcelona claim 28th LaLiga title after 2-0 win at Espanyol By Reuters It caps a remarkable first season at Barca for coach Hansi Flick as his side have won the LaLiga and Copa del Rey double.

By Reuters

By Reuters

By Reuters

By Reuters

By Reuters

Eight-time champions Germany have not won the tournament since 2013 in Stockholm, and in the meantime they have been reined in and passed by as other countries pour money into the development of the women’s game.

By Reuters

Agyemang is a skilled bulldozer of a striker with a nose for the net and no fear of battling for possession.

By Reuters

Sweden dismantled plenty of people’s favourites Germany 4-1 to top Group C and Williamson said they had not been getting the respect they deserved in Switzerland.

By Reuters

By Reuters

A record 48 teams will descend on co-hosts United States, Canada and Mexico next year for the quadrennial spectacle, with 104 matches on the schedule, up from just 64 the last time the tournament was held in 2022 in Qatar.

By Reuters

Following a bright start, capped with a goal from Jule Brand, Germany were second best for most of the match at Zurich’s Letzigrund stadium.

By Reuters

On Saturday, Wimbledon marked her love of the towels by presenting the new champion with a personalised purple and green version

By Reuters

Talks to renew the 2010 agreement stalled after India’s Supreme Court asked the AIFF not to renew their deal with FSDL until its order, the federation said.

By Reuters

There are many other plot lines but above all the showdown will help to cement a rivalry that could dominate tennis for a decade.
